example: a flea-bitten sofa; a woebegone old shack; a decrepit bus...its seats held together with friction tape; a creaky shack; his sorrow...made him look...haggard and...woebegone; a run-down neighborhood
synonyms: creaky, decrepit, derelict, flea-bitten, run-down, woeful

definition: an established custom
example: it was their habit to dine at 7 every evening

definition: humorously sarcastic or mocking; bent to one side
example: an ironic novel; a wry neck; an ironic remark often conveys an intended meaning obliquely; dry humor; with a wry Scottish wit; an ironical smile

definition: an involuntary intake of breath through a wide open mouth; usually triggered by fatigue or boredom; utter a yawn, as from lack of oxygen or when one is tired; with the mouth wide open indicating boredom or sleepiness; showing lack of attention or boredom; be wide open; gaping open as if threatening to engulf someone or something
example: a yawning abyss; The child yawned during the long performance; the yawning mine shaft; a yawning congregation; the yawning in the audience told him it was time to stop; the yawning congregation; the deep gaping canyon; he could not suppress a yawn; he apologized for his oscitancy
synonyms: drowsy, gape, oscitance, oscitancy, oscitant, yaw, yawn
